title: Artifact Signing Chain Enforcement
description: A build-and-CI governance workflow that enforces stage gates, capacity policies, and quality/security controls before pipeline advancement. Focus case: artifact signing chain enforcement.
assumptions: Signing keys and ephemeral worker tokens are single-use or explicitly consumed and must be re-provisioned after use.
Attestors and auditors are accountable identities; their actions are trusted for the purpose of state transitions.
Tool availability resources (test suites, scanners) are modeled as consumable bindings rather than unlimited parallel runs.
risks: Stale or compromised signing keys could produce fraudulent attestations that bypass gates.
Race conditions when returning/consuming capacity tokens (ci_worker or attestation_job) could allow double-allocation.
Over-reliance on automated policy assertions without human review may miss context-specific risks.
Insufficient evidence retention for countersignatures could prevent forensic audit or rollback.
